title: Silky Hummus
description: My Thermomix take on Ottolenghi's famously silky hummus — the smoothest dip you'll ever make.
intro: Ottolenghi's secret to glassy-smooth hummus is overcooked chickpeas and ice-cold water — and the Thermomix nails both. This is the version I make on repeat: rich, lemony and impossibly silky.
ingredients: Olive oil and paprika, to serve
steps: Place garlic in the mixing bowl and chop 3 sec / speed 7. Scrape down.
Add the drained chickpeas, bicarbonate of soda and 600 g water. Cook 20 min / 100°C / speed 1 to soften the chickpeas right down, then drain and return the warm chickpeas to the bowl.
Add tahini, lemon juice and salt. Blend 1 min / speed 8, scraping once.
With the blades running on speed 6, pour the iced water through the lid and blend a further 30 sec / speed 7 until pale and silky.
Spoon into a bowl, swirl with the back of a spoon, drizzle with olive oil and dust with paprika.
tips: Overcooked chickpeas + iced water is the whole trick to that silky texture.
Tins are easy, but soaked dried chickpeas give an even smoother result.
Keeps 4 days covered in the fridge — bring to room temp before serving.

faq.q: How do you make hummus smooth in a Thermomix?
faq.a: Soften the chickpeas further with bicarb soda, then blend hot with tahini and finish with iced water at speed 7 — the contrast is what makes it silky.
Yes. The extra 20-minute cook with bicarb softens tinned chickpeas enough for a smooth, Ottolenghi-style finish.
